title: Slow performance for selecting many columns
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the situation
We have recently faced a situation where we need to analyze the data stored, and we need to get all columns (22 columns), for about 200 million records. Projections are setup and used, partitioning is done, but the query takes about 25 seconds to execute.
I know even running such a query is a bit odd, but I wanted to make sure whether such an execution duration is expected from ClickHouse (due to its Columnar design) or it's even better and I'm doing something wrong?
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
Server version: 25.7.3
### How to reproduce
```
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS logs
(22 columns with numeric, string and low cardinality strings)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(date)
ORDER BY (toDate(date), host_id)
TTL date + INTERVAL 90 DAY
SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192, min_bytes_for_wide_part = 0;
```
`SELECT * FROM logs WHERE host_id = 200 FORMAT NULL; -- for benchmarking`
### Expected performance
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS logs (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, date DATE, host_id INTEGER, message TEXT, status TEXT, value REAL, count INTEGER, created_at DATETIME, updated_at DATETIME, user_id INTEGER, error_code INTEGER, description TEXT, tags TEXT, metadata TEXT, priority TEXT, session_id TEXT, ip_address TEXT, region TEXT, duration INTEGER, response_time REAL, is_active INTEGER);

INSERT INTO logs (id, date, host_id, message, status, value, count, created_at, updated_at, user_id, error_code, description, tags, metadata, priority, session_id, ip_address, region, duration, response_time, is_active) VALUES (1, '2023-01-01', 200, 'Log message 1', 'success', 123.45, 10, '2023-01-01 10:00:00', '2023-01-01 10:00:00', 1, 0, 'No error', 'tag1,tag2', 'key1:value1', 'medium', 'generated-uuid-1', '192.168.1.1', 'us-west', 100, 0.123, 1);

INSERT INTO logs (id, date, host_id, message, status, value, count, created_at, updated_at, user_id, error_code, description, tags, metadata, priority, session_id, ip_address, region, duration, response_time, is_active) VALUES (2, '2023-01-01', 200, 'Log message 2', 'error', 67.89, 5, '2023-01-01 10:01:00', '2023-01-01 10:01:00', 2, 404, 'Not found', 'tag3', 'key2:value2', 'high', 'generated-uuid-2', '192.168.1.2', 'us-east', 200, 0.456, 0);
SELECT * FROM logs WHERE host_id = 200 AND value > 50;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM logs WHERE status = 'error' AND created_at >= '2023-01-01 00:00:00' AND created_at < '2023-02-01 00:00:00';
key_bug_features: Selecting many columns from large datasets
